title: India-US Trade Talks: 'Substantial Progress' Noted After USTR Greer's Two-Day Delhi Visit
summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer concluded a two-day visit to New Delhi, with India and the US noting 'substantial progress' in bilateral trade deal negotiations. Multiple rounds of talks covered market access, non-tariff barriers, and pathways to an interim trade deal. No final deal was announced, but both sides acknowledged forward movement. The discussions come amid a looming US tariff deadline and pressure from Section 301 investigations.
exam_angle: India-US trade relations are a recurring GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) theme; key concepts include non-tariff barriers, bilateral trade agreements, and Section 301 of the US Trade Act.
key_facts: USTR: Jamieson Greer visited New Delhi (two-day visit, June 2026)
Key issues: market access, non-tariff barriers, interim trade deal pathway
No final deal concluded; 'substantial progress' noted jointly
Section 301 of US Trade Act and tariff deadline are pressure points

title: Venezuela Declares Emergency After Back-to-Back Earthquakes; India Offers Assistance
summary: Venezuela was struck by two powerful back-to-back earthquakes, causing building collapses in the capital Caracas. At least 32 people were killed and over 700 injured, with La Guaira declared a disaster zone by Vice President Delcy Rodriguez. Venezuela declared a state of emergency. India offered assistance to the quake-devastated country, reflecting its 'Neighbourhood First' and humanitarian outreach policy.
exam_angle: Relevant for Places in News (Venezuela, Caracas, La Guaira) and India's foreign policy of humanitarian diplomacy; also connects to disaster management and seismically active regions.
key_facts: Death toll: at least 32; injured: 700+
Epicentre region: Caracas and La Guaira declared disaster zone
Venezuela declared state of emergency
India offered assistance — reflects India's humanitarian diplomacy

title: MEA Clarifies: Indian Passport is a Travel Document, Not Proof of Citizenship
summary: A senior Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) official clarified that an Indian passport is a travel document and does not constitute proof of citizenship under Indian law. Citizenship in India is governed by the Citizenship Act, 1955, and the Constitution (Articles 5–11), not by possession of a passport. The clarification comes amid ongoing debates around citizenship, NRC, and CAA implementation.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to Polity: citizenship provisions under Articles 5–11 of the Constitution and the Citizenship Act, 1955; also links to NRC/CAA debates.
key_facts: Passport governed by: Passports Act, 1967 — a travel document only
Citizenship governed by: Articles 5–11 of Constitution and Citizenship Act, 1955
Statement by: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) senior official
Context: ongoing CAA/NRC debates on proof of citizenship

title: PM Modi Marks 'Samvidhan Hatya Diwas' on 51st Anniversary of Emergency (June 25)
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and BJP leaders observed June 25 as 'Samvidhan Hatya Diwas' (Constitution Murder Day), marking the 51st anniversary of the Emergency imposed by Prime Minister Indira Gandhi in 1975. PM Modi called the Emergency a 'direct assault on the Constitution' and described it as a period when Indian democracy was 'brutally crushed'. The Emergency (1975–77) involved suspension of Fundamental Rights and the 42nd Constitutional Amendment.
exam_angle: The Emergency of 1975 is a critical polity topic: suspension of Fundamental Rights, Article 352, 42nd Amendment, Kesavananda Bharati case context, and ADM Jabalpur case.
key_facts: June 25 observed as 'Samvidhan Hatya Diwas' by BJP government
Emergency declared on June 25, 1975 under Article 352 by PM Indira Gandhi
Emergency period: 1975–1977; 42nd Amendment passed during this period
Key cases: ADM Jabalpur (1976) — suspended Fundamental Rights during Emergency
source: The Times of India / The Hindu

title: Kerala High Court Invalidates BJP Councillors' Oaths Taken in Names of Deities and 'Mother India'
summary: The Kerala High Court invalidated the oaths of BJP councillors who had taken their oaths invoking deities and 'Mother India' instead of the constitutionally prescribed form. The court held that oaths must be taken only 'in the name of God' or by solemn affirmation as prescribed by the Constitution and relevant statutes. This ruling reinforces the constitutional requirement for secular, prescribed oath-taking by public officials.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of Third Schedule of the Constitution (forms of oaths), Articles 84, 99, 173, and constitutional secularism in public office.
key_facts: Court: Kerala High Court
Issue: BJP councillors took oaths invoking deities/'Mother India' — held invalid
Constitutional provision: Third Schedule prescribes forms of oaths/affirmations
Ruling: oaths must strictly follow constitutionally prescribed form

title: CBI Conducts Searches at Over 80 Locations Across India in Digital Arrest Scam Crackdown
summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) conducted coordinated searches at over 80 locations across India as part of a nationwide crackdown on 'digital arrest' cyber scams. Digital arrest scams involve fraudsters impersonating law enforcement officials and coercing victims into staying 'digitally arrested' under threat of fake charges, extorting large sums. The operation signals escalating law enforcement response to cybercrime.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Internal Security/Cybercrime) and GS Paper 2 (Government institutions — CBI mandate); also connects to IT Act provisions and cyber fraud frameworks.
key_facts: Agency: CBI conducted searches at 80+ locations simultaneously
Crime type: 'Digital arrest' scams — fraudsters impersonate police/CBI/ED
Victims coerced into 'digital confinement' and extorted via online calls
PM Modi had earlier publicly warned citizens about digital arrest scams

title: NIA: Pahalgam Terror Plot Was in Motion from April 15 Reconnaissance Trip
summary: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) revealed that the Pahalgam terror attack plot was in motion at least a week before the attack, with a reconnaissance trip conducted by the attackers on April 15. This provides new intelligence about the premeditated nature of the attack, which killed 26 tourists in Baisaran meadow, Pahalgam, Jammu & Kashmir. NIA's findings are central to India's ongoing counter-terrorism investigation and diplomatic pressure on Pakistan.
exam_angle: Connects to internal security, NIA's role under National Investigation Agency Act 2008, counter-terrorism policy, and India-Pakistan relations post-Pahalgam.
key_facts: Agency investigating: National Investigation Agency (NIA)
Reconnaissance trip: April 15, 2026 — over a week before the attack
Pahalgam attack: 26 tourists killed at Baisaran meadow, J&K
NIA Act 2008 governs NIA jurisdiction over terror cases

title: Air India Flight Briefly Enters Pakistani Airspace Near Amritsar; Pilot Taken Off Duty
summary: An Air India flight briefly and inadvertently entered Pakistani airspace near Amritsar, triggering a probe by aviation authorities. The pilot has been taken off duty pending the investigation. This incident is significant given the closure of Pakistani airspace to Indian carriers following the Pahalgam attack and the resulting suspension of India-Pakistan air links. Separately, it was reported that 3 Pakistani flights had entered Indian airspace days before this incident.
exam_angle: Relevant to India-Pakistan relations, airspace sovereignty, DGCA regulatory oversight, and the broader security context post-Pahalgam attack.
key_facts: Incident: Air India flight briefly entered Pakistani airspace near Amritsar
Action: Pilot taken off duty pending investigation by aviation authorities
Context: Pakistani airspace closed to Indian carriers since Pahalgam attack (April 2026)
Parallel: 3 Pakistani flights reportedly entered Indian airspace days earlier

title: BRICS MSME Forum 2026: Inaugural Session Calls for Stronger Collaboration and Global Integration
summary: The inaugural BRICS MSME Forum 2026 brought together BRICS nations to deliberate on stronger MSME collaboration, sustainability, and global integration. The forum focused on reducing barriers for micro, small and medium enterprises across BRICS economies and aligning MSME development with the UN Sustainable Development Goals. India's MSME sector, contributing ~30% to GDP, was highlighted as a model for inclusive growth.
exam_angle: BRICS forums and India's MSME policy are key GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) themes; MSME Ministry's initiatives and BRICS cooperation mechanisms are frequently examined.
key_facts: Event: Inaugural BRICS MSME Forum 2026
Focus areas: MSME collaboration, sustainability, global integration
India's MSME sector: ~30% of GDP, largest employer after agriculture
Nodal ministry: Ministry of MSME, Government of India

title: Iran Invites PM Modi to Ayatollah Khamenei's Funeral; West Asia Diplomacy in Focus
summary: Iran invited Prime Minister Narendra Modi to attend the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, signalling India's importance as a diplomatic partner in West Asia. The development comes amid ongoing US-Iran nuclear negotiations and India's delicate balancing act between its ties with Iran (Chabahar Port, energy) and its strategic partnership with the US and Gulf states. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio separately met Gulf leaders pledging security assurances amid Iran deal doubts.
exam_angle: India-Iran relations (Chabahar Port, energy security), India's West Asia policy, and the Iran nuclear deal are critical IR topics for GS Paper 2.
key_facts: Iran invited PM Modi to Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
India-Iran ties anchored by: Chabahar Port agreement, energy imports
US-Iran nuclear talks ongoing; Strait of Hormuz oil flows at record levels
US Secretary Rubio met Gulf leaders pledging security amid Iran deal uncertainty
source: The Times of India / The Hindu

title: UAE Interest in BrahMos and Akashteer Puts India's Defence Technology in Global Focus
summary: The UAE has expressed significant interest in India's defence technologies, including the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile and the Akashteer automated air defence control and reporting system developed by DRDO/BEL. This reflects growing demand for Indian defence exports following the Philippines' successful BrahMos acquisition and India's push under the 'Make in India' defence exports policy. India targets $5 billion in defence exports by 2025.
exam_angle: India's defence exports policy, BrahMos (India-Russia JV), Akashteer (DRDO/BEL), and the $5 billion defence export target are key GS Paper 3 (Defence/Economy) topics.
key_facts: BrahMos: India-Russia joint venture supersonic cruise missile; range ~290–800 km
Akashteer: Automated Air Defence Control & Reporting System by DRDO/BEL
UAE expressed interest in both systems
India's defence export target: USD 5 billion by 2025 under Make in India

title: Sarvam AI Receives Government Backing; Amazon CEO Andy Jassy Visits India
summary: Indian AI startup Sarvam AI received state backing, marking a significant step in India's push for indigenous large language model (LLM) development focused on Indian languages. Separately, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy visited India, stating that 'lessons from India are helping us across the world', underscoring India's importance as an innovation hub. These developments align with India's National AI Mission and the government's Digital India push.
exam_angle: India's National AI Mission (INDIAai), government support for indigenous AI, and Digital India are important GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) topics.
key_facts: Sarvam AI: Indian startup focused on Indian-language LLMs; receives government/state backing
National AI Mission (INDIAai): Launched 2024; budget Rs 10,371 crore
Amazon CEO Andy Jassy visited India; highlighted India as global innovation model
Context: India-UAE tech pact also seen as challenging dominance of US tech giants

title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Illicitly Accessing Claude AI Model — Largest Known AI Theft Attempt
summary: US-based AI company Anthropic accused Chinese tech giant Alibaba of 'illicitly' accessing its Claude AI models, citing approximately 28.8 million unauthorised queries — described as the largest known AI model theft attempt. Anthropic alleged this was an effort to 'distil' its proprietary Claude model. The incident highlights growing concerns about AI intellectual property protection and US-China technology competition, with the US simultaneously restricting access to advanced AI models.
exam_angle: Relevant to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech): AI governance, intellectual property in AI, and US-China technology rivalry; also connects to India's positioning in global AI policy.
key_facts: Accuser: Anthropic (US AI company, maker of Claude AI)
Accused: Alibaba (Chinese tech conglomerate)
Scale: ~28.8 million unauthorised Claude queries — largest known AI theft attempt
US is simultaneously restricting access to advanced AI models (Mythos, Fable 5)

title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil Custom AI Chip; Qualcomm to Acquire Modular for $3.9 Billion
summary: OpenAI and Broadcom jointly unveiled a custom AI chip designed to reduce dependence on Nvidia's GPU infrastructure for AI workloads. Separately, Qualcomm announced the acquisition of AI software company Modular for $3.9 billion, signalling consolidation in the AI hardware-software ecosystem. These developments are part of a global race to build sovereign AI chip capabilities, relevant to India's semiconductor and AI policy ambitions.
exam_angle: Connects to GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech): semiconductor policy, India Semiconductor Mission, AI chip geopolitics, and India's push for chip manufacturing under the PLI scheme.
key_facts: OpenAI + Broadcom: unveiled custom AI chip to reduce Nvidia GPU dependence
Qualcomm acquires Modular (AI software) for USD 3.9 billion
India's semiconductor push: India Semiconductor Mission with Rs 76,000 crore outlay
Geopolitical angle: US-China chip war; India positioning as alternative fab destination

title: Colombia Presidential Election: Trump-Endorsed Miguel de la Espriella Wins Runoff
summary: Miguel de la Espriella, endorsed by former US President Donald Trump, won Colombia's presidential runoff election, marking a shift in the country's political direction. Colombia is a significant economy in Latin America and a key US partner in counter-narcotics operations. The election result could affect Colombia's relations with neighbouring Venezuela (which declared an emergency after earthquakes) and regional geopolitics in Latin America.
exam_angle: Latin American political developments, US foreign policy influence, and regional geopolitics are relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) — places in news and international relations.
key_facts: Winner: Miguel de la Espriella — Trump-endorsed candidate
Election type: Presidential runoff, Colombia
Colombia: major Latin American economy; key US counter-narcotics partner
Geopolitical context: Venezuela emergency, regional political shifts in Latin America

title: 'Omega' Heatwave Grips Western Europe, Shattering Temperature Records
summary: A deadly 'Omega' heatwave — named after the Omega blocking weather pattern that traps hot air — gripped Western Europe, shattering temperature records in France, UK, and Spain. The Omega blocking pattern occurs when a high-pressure system stalls over a region, preventing cooler air from moving in. Scientists linked the intensifying frequency and severity of European heatwaves to climate change and global warming, raising concerns about climate inequality and urban heat islands.
exam_angle: Omega blocking pattern, urban heat islands, climate change-induced extreme weather events, and IPCC findings are key GS Paper 3 (Environment) and GS Paper 1 (Geography) topics.
key_facts: 'Omega' heatwave: caused by Omega blocking pattern (stalled high-pressure system)
Countries most affected: France, UK, Spain — all recording unprecedented temperatures
Climate science link: IPCC warns heatwaves will become more frequent/intense with 1.5°C+ warming
Urban heat island effect amplifies heatwave severity in cities

title: Bonded Labour Rescued in Uttar Pradesh: 13 Workers Branded, Whipped and Guarded by Pitbulls
summary: Authorities in Uttar Pradesh rescued 13 bonded labourers from a factory where they were reportedly branded, whipped, and guarded by pitbull dogs — a case of extreme violation of human dignity and constitutional rights. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi condemned the incident as an 'assault on human dignity'. The case highlights the continued existence of bonded labour despite the Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act, 1976.
exam_angle: Bonded labour is prohibited under Article 23 of the Constitution (Right against Exploitation); the Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act, 1976 is a key statutory provision for GS Paper 2 (Polity/Social Justice).
key_facts: Location: Uttar Pradesh factory
Victims: 13 bonded labourers — branded, whipped, guarded by dogs
Constitutional provision: Article 23 prohibits forced labour and human trafficking
Statutory law: Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act, 1976

title: India Financial Crime Agency (ED) Flags Forex Violations and Missing Records at Rajesh Exports
summary: India's Enforcement Directorate (ED) flagged foreign exchange violations and missing financial records at Rajesh Exports, one of India's largest gold jewellery exporters. The action relates to alleged contraventions of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), 1999. Rajesh Exports is listed on Indian stock exchanges and has been a significant player in the gems and jewellery export sector.
exam_angle: FEMA (Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999), the ED's mandate under PMLA and FEMA, and India's forex management framework are important GS Paper 3 (Economy) topics.
key_facts: Agency: Enforcement Directorate (ED)
Company: Rajesh Exports — major gold jewellery exporter listed on Indian bourses
Violations: Foreign exchange breaches and missing financial records
Governing law: Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), 1999
